edges.description: Defective BTK signaling blocks progression of B-lineage precursors, arresting development at the pre-B stage.
edges.source: BTK Loss of Function Impairs B-Cell Receptor Signaling
edges.target: Arrest of B-Cell Development at the Pre-B Stage

edges.description: The pre-B developmental block leaves very few mature B cells in the circulation.
edges.source: Arrest of B-Cell Development at the Pre-B Stage

edges.description: Absence of mature B cells and plasma cells prevents immunoglobulin production, producing panhypogammaglobulinemia.
edges.source: Arrest of B-Cell Development at the Pre-B Stage

edges.description: Loss of protective antibody predisposes to recurrent infection with encapsulated bacteria.
intermediate_mechanisms: Absent antibody removes humoral protection against encapsulated bacteria.

edges.description: Loss of neutralizing antibody leaves XLA patients unable to control enteroviral infection, predisposing to severe disseminated enteroviral disease.
intermediate_mechanisms: Absent neutralizing antibody removes humoral control of enteroviruses.

nodes.description: The central lesion of XLA is failure of pre-B cells in the bone marrow to develop into circulating mature B cells. This developmental block produces a severe deficit of peripheral B cells.
nodes.id: Arrest of B-Cell Development at the Pre-B Stage

evidence.reference_title: The gene involved in X-linked agammaglobulinaemia is a member of the src family of protein-tyrosine kinases.
evidence.snippet: failure of pre-B cells in the bone marrow to develop into circulating mature B cells

nodes.description: BTK (Bruton tyrosine kinase) is a cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase expressed throughout the B lineage that transduces signals from the pre-B-cell and mature B-cell receptors. Loss-of-function BTK variants reduce or abolish kinase expression and activity, disabling the signaling pathways required for B-cell expansion and maturation.
nodes.id: BTK Loss of Function Impairs B-Cell Receptor Signaling

evidence.reference_title: Deficient expression of a B cell cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase in human X-linked agammaglobulinemia.
evidence.snippet: Reduction in or the absence of BPK mRNA, protein expression, and kinase activity was observed in XLA pre-B and B cell lines.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of unique region of Bruton's tyrosine kinase in immunodeficient XID mice.
evidence.snippet: Loss of kinase activity results in the human immunodeficiency, X-linked agammaglobulinemia, characterized by a failure to produce B cells.

evidence.reference_title: Deficient expression of a B cell cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase in human X-linked agammaglobulinemia.
evidence.snippet: an inherited immunodeficiency characterized by a severe deficit of B and plasma cells and profound hypogammaglobulinemia

nodes.description: Without mature B cells to differentiate into antibody-secreting plasma cells, immunoglobulin production fails across all isotypes, producing profound hypogammaglobulinemia (markedly reduced IgG, IgA, and IgM).

evidence.reference_title: Agammaglobulinemia: from X-linked to Autosomal Forms of Disease.
evidence.snippet: Interruptions or alterations in the B cell development pathway can lead to primary B cell immunodeficiency with resultant absence or diminished immunoglobulin production

evidence.reference_title: X-linked agammaglobulinemia: report on a United States registry of 201 patients.
evidence.snippet: Infection was the most common initial clinical presentation (85%), followed by a positive family history (41%) and neutropenia (11%).

nodes.description: Beyond pyogenic bacterial infection, the absence of neutralizing antibody gives XLA patients a characteristic vulnerability to severe and disseminated enteroviral disease (including chronic meningoencephalitis), historically a leading cause of mortality.

evidence.reference_title: X-linked agammaglobulinemia: report on a United States registry of 201 patients.
evidence.snippet: Causes of death included disseminated enterovirus infection (n = 6)
Severe, difficult-to-treat enteroviral infections (often manifesting as dermatomyositis or chronic meningoencephalitis) can be prevented by this treatment.
